(4)

item.dc:creator: PineappleIsDeliciousOnPizzaFightMe

item.title: It's Never Too Late
item.description: – At age 23, Tina Fey was working at a YMCA. – At age 23, Oprah was fired from her first reporting job. – At age 24, Stephen King was working as a janitor and living in a trailer. – At age 27, Vincent Van Gogh failed as a missionary and decided to go to art school. – At age 28, J.K. Rowling was a suicidal single parent living on welfare. – At age 28, Wayne Coyne (from The Flaming Lips) was a fry cook. – At age 30, Harrison Ford was a carpenter. – At age 30, Martha Stewart was a stockbroker. – At age 37, Ang Lee was a stay-at-home-dad working odd jobs. – Julia Child released her first cookbook at age 49. – Vera Wang failed to make the Olympic figure skating team, didn’t get the Editor-in-Chief position at Vogue, and designed her first dress at age 40. – Stan Lee didn’t release his first big comic book until he was 38. – Alan Rickman gave up his graphic design career to pursue acting at age 42. – Samuel L. Jackson didn’t get his first major movie role until he was 41. – Morgan Freeman landed his first MAJOR movie role at age 52. – Kathryn Bigelow only reached international success when she made The Hurt Locker at age 57. – Grandma Moses didn’t begin her painting career until age 76. – Louise Bourgeois wasn’t featured in the Museum of Modern Art until she was 71. At 37 I was on parole after a 3rd DUI and decided to finally start college. At 47, I was a licensed mental health counselor, working in Hawaii, with my own private practice. It's not too late.
item.dc:creator: SillyPhilosopher

item.title: The basket star, "Gorgonocephalus eucnemis", is basically a fancy brittle star,
item.description: After attaching to a rock or other firm substrate, an adult basket star will spread it's intricately branched arms into the water to catch tiny zooplankton (crustaceans, arrow worms), and sometimes fish larvae and jellies. live in deep sea habitats or cold waters, though some basket stars can be seen at night in shallow tropical reefs. Most young basket stars live on specific type of coral. In the wild they may live up to 35 years.
item.dc:creator: dolphinyaso

item.title: Josh Hawley tryna fundraise after being embarrassed at J6 Hearing
item.description: ICYMI: Surveillance footage of Joshy-poo running away like a scared lil bitch, was shown during the January 6th hearing last night, eliciting audible snickers from people in the Chamber. item.title: Starbucks gets its 200th Unionized Store!
item.description: With the two new Cleveland Starbucks voting to unionize, Starbucks now has 200 unionized locations in the US. With 15,000 stores in the United States, there are still many more to go, but this is a huge milestone and a great start! Cleveland : https://www.clevescene.com/news/two-more-cleveland-area-starbucks-stores-vote-to-unionize-39352825 Tweet link : https://twitter.com/greenhousenyt/status/1550553379675439105?t=2u9Tee82wdYnrC2bTu2Tzw&s=19 More info on the Starbucks Union and its efforts : https://sbworkersunited.org/new-page-2
item.dc:creator: Orzufancylad

item.title: The ferry I was on caught fire and ran aground. Safely off now!
item.description:
The ferry is from Nova Scotia to PEI in Canada. Dark smoke started billowing up, from the engines supposedly. It smelled like diesel.
We were mustered into the cafeteria while the fire alarm was going off. Power went out.
You could see smoke spreading out across the water.
Soon we were on deck waiting to evacuate.
Smoke kept coming, but not quite so much.
Some of the crew were pretty good. The guy who kept walking around saying “keep calm!” (not the guy shown) not so much.
The lowered the inflatable thing to evacuate people. People on shore got in their lobster boats and pleasure craft to come out and help evacuate.
Some people were going “wheeee!” as they went down.
The inflatable thing was taking on a bit of water - I’d say 2 or 3 inches in the centre. But a lobster boat came alongside fast to pick us up.
People were generally in good spirits. Old people, kids, pets, etc, all seemed to be gotten off safely and calmly.
I was not the only one taking pictures.
This dog made it off! It doesn’t seem happy though.
The unloading of the inflatable thing took a bit of doing. It was a big step up into the lobster boat, and some people needed help.
We were soon on our way away.
The boat doesn’t look happy.
Certainly the most interesting trip I’ve had in a while.
We came off on the dock.
The life vests were soon stacked. I wanted to keep mine.
Boats kept coming in from the ship. I think that these might be crew.
As I was loaded on to the bus I took a few pictures of a helicopter that flew in. I’m now in the visitors centre waiting to get my name and details taken. There’s a TV here showing a static ad for the ferry: “Travel by sea. Save time. Drive less.” I’m giggling a bit to see it.
item.dc:creator: mrchalifax
